description:
https://krisp-khaki.vercel.app/admin/integrations  

Add a settings page so that the webhook can authenticate using the webhook secret. I believe we need to provide an admin page to allow the user to kind of figure this. 

In Krisp.ai, their integration page shows the Request Header authorization secret.
title: Implement webhook authentication settings page

summary:
## Summary: Implement webhook authentication settings page

### Changes Implemented
- Added `webhook_secrets` database table with per-user secret storage, FK to users with cascade delete, and unique index on (user_id, name) for multi-integration support
- Created `/api/webhook-secret` API route with GET (returns masked secret), POST (generates/regenerates secret via atomic upsert), and DELETE (revokes secret) - all protected by NextAuth session auth
- Updated `/api/webhooks/key-points` route to validate incoming webhook Authorization headers against per-user secrets from the database, with fallback to the global `WEBHOOK_SECRET` env var
- Replaced hand-rolled timing comparison with Node.js `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for proper timing-attack resistance
- Built `WebhookSecretManager` interactive component in the Krisp Meetings tab of the Integrations page: generate, copy, regenerate, and revoke webhook secrets with proper masked display and one-time full secret visibility after generation
- Updated Krisp setup guide Step 3 to reference the generated secret instead of the old env var approach
- Generated and pushed database migration (`0001_melodic_solo.sql`)

### Files Modified
- `lib/db/schema.ts` - Added `webhookSecrets` table definition
- `app/api/webhook-secret/route.ts` - **NEW** - CRUD API for managing webhook secrets
- `app/api/webhooks/key-points/route.ts` - Updated auth to use per-user secrets with global fallback
- `app/(app)/admin/integrations/IntegrationsClient.tsx` - Added `WebhookSecretManager` component, updated setup guide
- `drizzle/migrations/0001_melodic_solo.sql` - **NEW** - Database migration for webhook_secrets table

### Notes for Developer
- The secret is shown in full only once after generation - users must copy it immediately before navigating away
- If no per-user secret is configured, the webhook route falls back to the `WEBHOOK_SECRET` environment variable, then to allowing unauthenticated access (with a console warning)
- The `name` column in `webhook_secrets` defaults to "Krisp" but supports future expansion to other integrations (Gmail, Microsoft 365, etc.)
- The generated secret is 64 hex characters (256-bit entropy via `crypto.randomBytes`)
- In Krisp.ai's webhook configuration, the user should paste the generated secret as the `Authorization` request header value
